file_insights.details: Added 'Storage — HARD RULES' section codifying non-negotiable architectural constraints around storage layer. Section explicitly rejects SQLite, Dolt, and custom databases in favor of filesystem state machine + JSONL event log model.

patterns_discovered.description: Use of strong, unambiguous language ('do not re-litigate', 'do not sneak', 'you are wrong') to enforce architectural decisions and prevent future regressions
patterns_discovered.context: Storage decisions that have been thoroughly debated and must not resurface in team discussions

patterns_discovered.description: Explicit enumeration of prohibited technologies (rusqlite, sqlx, sled, redb, etc.) rather than principle-only guidance
patterns_discovered.context: Prevents developers from proposing alternatives that technically fit the described constraints

patterns_discovered.description: Hard rules section references detailed rationale document (docs/storage-decision.md) for deeper understanding
patterns_discovered.context: Balances brevity of constraint with access to full decision context

gotchas_discovered.description: Architectural decisions, even when thoroughly decided, will be repeatedly proposed by team members unless explicitly documented as non-negotiable
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Include 'hard rules' sections in team documentation with explicit 'do not re-open' language

gotchas_discovered.description: Without documented hard rules, developers waste effort proposing storage alternatives, leading to unproductive discussions

approach_outcome.test_results: make check passed (test + clippy + fmt all green)
approach_outcome.delivery: Single commit codifying storage layer decision with rationale and prohibition list

recommendations.rationale: Other architectural decisions (VM-first dispatch, Nushell canonicalization, jj-only VCS) could benefit from similar hard-rule documentation with explicit rejection lists
Include 'Refs: CLAUDE.md Storage HARD RULES' in commits touching storage to maintain traceability
Consider similar sections for: VCS workflow (jj only), shell scripting (Nushell only), deployment model (VM-first only)
